titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.24

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=640 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:24:06.699768+00 | translation moa=641 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:24:04.941082+00

p.cl:
Vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis Alanus filius Walteri dapifer Regis Scott’ .
salutem .

me dedisse et concessisse et hac mea carta
confirmasse deo et Sancte MARIE et Sancto Benedicto et monachis de CUPRE .
unum plenum toftum in burgo meo de REINFRIV iuxta cimeterium . et
unum rethe ad Salmonem in clud habendum et possidendum in perpetuam he
lemosinam . sicut liberius . quietius . et honorificentius aliquam elemosinam pos
sident monachi ordinis cisternensis . pro anima regis Dauid . et REGIS Malcol
mi . et comitis Henrici . et pro anima patris mei et matris mee . et pro salute ani
me mee et antecessorum meorum .

Hanc autem elemosinam ego Alanus et here
des mei monachis et ab eis tenentibus in perpetuum warantizabimus .

His
Testibus . Episcopo de Glasgv . Priore Osberto de Passel’ et conuentu . Willelmo de fonte
capellano . Ricardo persona de Dvndov . Willelmo de Passel’ . fulcone clerico . Roberto croc .
Gilleberto filio Swein . Ric’ Ruhefd . Wlbrano . Reginaldo modesune . Edulfo Waite .

div.p: To all Holy Mother Church's sons, Alan son of Walter, steward of the king of Scots, [sends] greeting. Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, St Mary and St Benedict, and to the monks of Coupar, one full toft in my burgh of Renfrew beside the churchyard, and one net for salmon in the Clyde; to have and possess in everlasting alms just as the monks of the Cistercian Order freely, quitly, and honourably possess any alms; for the soul of King David, and of King Malcolm, and of Earl Henry, and for the soul of my father and of my mother, and for the salvation of my own soul and of my predecessors'. This alms I, Alan, and my heirs, shall warrant for ever to the monks and to those holding of them. With these as witnesses: the bishop of Glasgow; Prior Osbert of Paisley and the convent; William of Wells, chaplain; Richard, parson of Dundov; William of Paisley; Fulk, clerk; Robert Crock; Gilbert son of Swein; Richard Ruhefd; Wulfbrand; Reginald Mothsune; Eadwulf Waite.
